description: Deleting one of several byte-identical functions must retire ITS handle, so a byte-identical sibling can never be reached through the dead handle. Built on a throwaway rw worktree off forgeql-pub.frozen; handles are captured at runtime, never hard-coded. The whole-node delete is correct today and gates hard. The offset-delete case pins a known leak and runs SOFT (visible, non-gating) until the removal-range fix lands, at which point it is promoted to hard.

steps.fql: INSERT AFTER NODE '${F}' WITH 'void dup() {} // FIRST'
INSERT AFTER NODE '${F}' WITH 'void dup() {} // SECOND'
INSERT AFTER NODE '${F}' WITH 'void dup() {} // THIRD'
FIND symbols WHERE name = 'dup' IN 'golden_twins.cpp' ORDER BY line
INSERT AFTER NODE '${F}' WITH 'void dup() {} // FIRST'
INSERT AFTER NODE '${F}' WITH 'void dup() {} // SECOND'
INSERT AFTER NODE '${F}' WITH 'void dup() {} // THIRD'
FIND symbols WHERE name = 'dup' IN 'golden_twins.cpp' ORDER BY line
